title: left-over files from deleting a frozen container
body:
### Issue Description

Every now and then, I encounter an container that in a limbo state.
It's neither running nor being listed with `podman container ls --all` but it's name can't be re-used either.
Typically, the podman process was interrupted using ctrl-c before but then failed to terminate correctly.
It seems to happen most with the postgres container.

```
time="2024-03-11T21:06:04Z" level=warning msg="Unmounting container \"postgres\" while attempting to delete storage: unmounting \"/home/postgres/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/6e06361057c3ef4ad4537ab6c9a19647817ff7e0e387f5a3da1589fe87349103/merged\": invalid argument"
```

A `sudo rm -rf /home/postgres/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/6e06361057c3ef4ad4537ab6c9a19647817ff7e0e387f5a3da1589fe87349103` appears to suffice for recovery.

Maybe related to https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12316?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

- run `podman run --rm --name "postgres" -p 5432:5432 --userns keep-id --hostuser=postgres --env-file /home/postgres/postgres.env -v "some/dir/for/postgres:/var/lib/postgresql/data" docker.io/library/postgres:14-alpine`
- connect an active client
- send sigint (ctrl-c) to podman
- sometimes, it doesn't react
  - great, the reproduction is working
  - `podman rm --ignore -f -t 10 "postgres"`
- try step 1 again
  - if in the error state, `podman run` won't work (either because of the name not being available or due to failed cleanup, see further down)
  - in that case, stop :wink: 

### Describe the results you received

There are still files related to the removed container, like:

```
$ cat overlay-containers/volatile-containers.json 
[{"id":"564aef3136169972e10c828f2971584dcc8a0de584a7237265585dbb85e4978e","names":["postgres"],"image":"54ae7e6e096bdd77229713bb3a17983e82b7def419a0a78ef4b1102478515c0b","layer":"6e06361057c3ef4ad4537ab6c9a19647817ff7e0e387f5a3da1589fe87349103","metadata":"{\"image-name\":\"docker.io/library/postgres:14-alpine\",\"image-id\":\"54ae7e6e096bdd77229713bb3a17983e82b7def419a0a78ef4b1102478515c0b\",\"name\":\"postgres\",\"created-at\":1710172119}","created":"2024-03-11T15:48:39.069743504Z","uidmap":[{"container_id":0,"host_id":1,"size":1003},{"container_id":1003,"host_id":0,"size":1},{"container_id":1004,"host_id":1004,"size":64533}],"gidmap":[{"container_id":0,"host_id":1,"size":1003},{"container_id":1003,"host_id":0,"size":1},{"container_id":1004,"host_id":1004,"size":64533}],"flags":{"MountLabel":"","ProcessLabel":"","Volatile":true}}]
```

Some of those files belong to the first mapped user/group (`/etc/sub{u,g}id`) and can't be deleted without root:

```
drwx------ 4   296608   296608 4096 Mar 11 20:35 overlay-containers/564aef3136169972e10c828f2971584dcc8a0de584a7237265585dbb85e4978e/userdata
..
drwx------  5   296608   296608 4096 Mar 11 15:48 overlay/6e06361057c3ef4ad4537ab6c9a19647817ff7e0e387f5a3da1589fe87349103
```

### Describe the results you expected

All files relating to containers should be gone.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Rocky Linux 9.3

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: there were some improvements in this area since 4.6.1.  Please try with a newer version

Yes I am very sure this was fixed in 4.7
Duplicate of #19913
